title: put() ignores cached_nvm_id, pays O(n) scan per write
description: put() calls find_by_label() unconditionally, which calls nvm_list() + nvm_metadata() for every NVM object until the label is found. get() has a get_cached_or_scan() that validates then reuses the cached NVM ID. put() has no equivalent. For repeated writes to the same key (e.g., rotating a secret in a loop), this pays O(n_nvm_objects) round-trips per write instead of O(1). Fix: replicate the cache-validation pattern from get_cached_or_scan inside put() — check metadata of cached_nvm_id first, use it on match, fall back to scan on miss.
